user_query:
I’m polishing a short lab update and want to avoid repeating any misinformation. Please fact-check the statements in input/claims.jsonl and then rewrite my Slack note in input/draft_slack.txt based on your findings.

Requirements and deliverables:
1) Inspect the workspace and produce a simple inventory of files and folders (names only) to outputs/workspace_inventory.txt so I can see what you used.
2) For each claim in input/claims.jsonl:
   - Use a search engine to locate authoritative sources. Prefer official project materials (e.g., the Arakoon project repository or organization site under Open vStorage/Incubaid) and official Python documentation (e.g., docs.python.org) over blogs or forums.
   - Create a JSON evidence file at outputs/evidence/<claim_id>_sources.json containing an array of 1–3 objects with fields: title, source_domain, access_date (YYYY-MM-DD), and a short quoted snippet supporting your verdict. Include URLs in these JSON files if helpful, but do not include URLs anywhere else.
   - Add a row to outputs/verification.csv with columns: id, topic, claim, verdict, confidence, evidence_file. verdict must be one of {Supported, Refuted, Unclear}. confidence is a numeric value between 0 and 1 reflecting how strong the evidence is. evidence_file is the relative path to the JSON you created for that claim.
   - Log the search queries you actually used (one or more per claim) to outputs/search_queries.txt in any readable format.
3) Rewrite input/draft_slack.txt to outputs/rewritten_slack_message.txt with a clear, constructive tone and under 120 words. Structure it with two labeled sections: “Verified:” (facts you found Supported) and “Corrections:” (items that were Refuted or Unclear, phrased helpfully). Do not include URLs in this Slack message.

Notes:
- Do not rely on local assumptions; actually perform search engine queries to justify your verdicts.
- Keep outputs in the specified paths. Do not download large binaries; only reference textual sources.

Inputs: input/claims.jsonl, input/draft_slack.txt
Outputs: outputs/workspace_inventory.txt, outputs/evidence/<claim_id>_sources.json for each claim, outputs/verification.csv, outputs/search_queries.txt, outputs/rewritten_slack_message.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
